policy.ordering: by release, then id; respect blockedBy

features.title: Monorepo & toolchain scaffold (Turborepo + pnpm + TS strict + lint/format/test)
acceptance: pnpm-workspace + turbo.json + base tsconfig (strict) build an empty workspace
ESLint (type-aware) + Prettier + Vitest configured; package-boundary lint rule present
verification gates typecheck/lint/format/test/build run green on the scaffold
features.notes: Done 2026-06-28: gates active; @tessera/core shell stands up the toolchain (F-002 fleshes it out).

features.title: @tessera/core: ids, typed errors, config types, domain event bus
acceptance: Stable id helpers, typed error base classes, and result/error types
In-memory domain event bus with typed events
No internal dependencies; consumable by all packages
features.notes: Done 2026-06-28: id/errors/result/config/events + 15 tests; gates green.

features.title: Storage ports + SQLite & filesystem adapters + conformance suite
acceptance: RelationalStore + BlobStore + Queue ports defined (Drizzle for SQL)
SQLite, filesystem, and in-process queue adapters implemented
Shared conformance suite passes for every adapter
features.notes: Done 2026-06-28: 3 ports + 3 local adapters (sqlite via better-sqlite3+Drizzle, filesystem, in-process), each passing a conformance suite; 13 storage tests.

features.title: VectorStore port + sqlite-vec adapter (model+dim recorded per vector)
acceptance: VectorStore port with upsert/query and capability flags
sqlite-vec adapter passing the conformance suite
Each vector records its embedding model id + dimension
features.notes: Done 2026-06-28: VectorStore port + sqlite-vec adapter (vec0; model+dimension per vector) passing a 6-test conformance suite.

features.title: Embeddings port + Transformers.js adapter (Ollama optional)
acceptance: Embeddings port (embed/batch) with model metadata
Transformers.js in-process adapter works with zero external services/keys
Optional Ollama adapter behind the same port
features.notes: Done 2026-06-28: Embeddings port + transformers (live smoke-verified, all-MiniLM 384-d) + ollama (optional) + fake (deterministic, drives the conformance gate); real adapters' tests guarded by env.

features.title: Ingestion: local filesystem + Git, event-driven, incremental, secret-redacted
acceptance: Filesystem + Git connectors emit change events; workers process via Queue port
Incremental + idempotent (content-hash diffing); no full re-index on small changes
Secret scrubbing before persist; processors are plugins
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/ingestion — Connector/Processor/DocumentSink/IngestionManifest ports; filesystem + git (CLI, repo provenance) connectors; normalize + enforced-terminal redaction processors; content-hash diff → coordinator enqueues only changes → worker consumes via Queue port, idempotent/incremental. ADR-0015. 25 tests (connector conformance, fs + git integration, full pipeline lifecycle). Embedding/vector/graph persistence is the DocumentSink seam for F-007/8/9; full git history/diff/blame + fs.watch deferred.

features.title: Memory subsystem: types, metadata, versioning, manual capture
acceptance: Memory kinds (decision/lesson/incident/failure/architecture/glossary/task) with metadata
Versioned + supersedable (never silently mutated)
Create/edit via API + MCP
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/memory — 7 MemoryKinds + metadata; immutable version lineage (edit appends a superseding version, prior never mutated; FR-12); Zod-validated MemoryService (capture/edit/getCurrent/history/list) = the API/MCP-facing seam (F-011/F-012 wrap it); MemoryStore port + in-memory + sqlite (Drizzle over storage's handle, atomic supersede) adapters, both passing the conformance suite. 25 tests. Effect E-010. Auto-extraction (FR-14), retention (FR-15), migration tooling (F-024) deferred.

features.title: Knowledge graph + effect-links + get_effects
acceptance: Nodes/edges model (relational, recursive-CTE traversal) for files/symbols/modules/people/decisions
Effect-links typed with rationale/confidence/origin; static + manual
get_effects(symbol) returns ranked dependents with paths
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/knowledge-graph — node/edge model (file/symbol/module/person/decision/memory); typed effect-links (rationale/confidence/origin) asserted manually + derived statically by inverting import/call/reference edges (FR-18); get_effects = ranked, path-bearing traversal of dependents (FR-19). GraphStore port + in-memory (BFS) + sqlite (recursive CTE, cycle-guarded) adapters, both ranked through one shared selectBestRanked for parity; passing one conformance suite. Zod-validated service = API/MCP seam. 23 tests. Effects E-002/E-011. Symbol extraction to populate nodes (OQ5) is downstream ingestion.

features.title: Hybrid retrieval: semantic + keyword + graph + symbolic + fusion ranker
acceptance: Five retrievers behind a common interface; keyword via FTS, semantic via VectorStore
Fusion ranker with configurable weights and per-candidate signal attribution
Returns one ranked candidate set
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/retrieval — Retriever interface; semantic (VectorStore), keyword (SQLite FTS5), graph (KG seed+effect-expand), symbolic (exact symbol) retrievers; weighted Reciprocal-Rank-Fusion ranker (configurable per-signal weights, per-candidate signal attribution) → one ranked set; Zod-validated hybrid service (API/MCP seam). 23 tests. Effect E-012. NOTE: requirements are FR-21/22/23/25/26 (4 retrievers + fusion) — temporal (FR-24) is R1/F-018, the 5th behind the same interface; the acceptance's 'five' counts it. Consistent cross-backend ref space for fusion is an ingestion/config seam (downstream).

features.title: Context Compiler: plan→retrieve→expand→rank→dedup→compress→assemble + provenance
acceptance: Full pipeline producing a provenance-tagged, token-budget-bounded Context Package
Deduplication + per-fragment 'why included' explainability
Compilation trace emitted for the Package Inspector
Beats naive top-k RAG on the labeled task suite (Context Quality Score)
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/context-compiler — compile(task,budget,filters) runs plan→retrieve→expand(get_effects)→rank→resolve→dedup(shingle Jaccard)→compress(budget-fit, never exceeds)→assemble. Provenance-tagged, sectioned, budget-bounded ContextPackage + per-fragment whyIncluded (FR-32) + full CompilationTrace (stage drops) for the inspector. FragmentSource = corpus seam. CQS + naiveTopK baseline; beats-naive eval test passes (compiler > naive on relevance/redundancy/provenance/overall). 17 tests. Effects E-003/E-013. LLM compression (FR-31), reproducibility/caching + pluggable-per-stage (FR-33/34) are R1 (F-019/F-020).

features.title: REST API /v1 (Fastify): schema validation, OpenAPI, health/ready
acceptance: Versioned /v1 routes for search/compile/effects/memory; Zod validation at boundary
OpenAPI generated from schemas; consistent error envelope
/health and /ready endpoints
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: apps/api (@tessera/api) — Fastify v5 + fastify-type-provider-zod (Zod-v4 bridge: one schema drives validation+serialization+OpenAPI) + @fastify/swagger at GET /v1/openapi.json. Thin /v1 routes wrap the F-007..F-010 services: POST /v1/search, POST /v1/compile, GET /v1/effects, POST/GET/PATCH /v1/memory(/:lineageId[/history]). Consistent error envelope {error:{code,message,details?}} via one setErrorHandler (TesseraError.code->status; 5xx masked; Zod->400) + setNotFoundHandler. /health + /ready (injected readiness probe; 503 until ready). buildServer(services) is pure DI — the ApiServices composition seam F-015 fills with the real local profile (no shipped boot/toy wiring here); MCP (F-012) wraps the same services. e2e gate (gate 6) ACTIVATED: root test:e2e -> turbo; CI step added (effect E-005); 14 app.inject e2e + 11 unit (error-map + schema). ADR-0016. Effect E-003 realized (REST half). Auth/CORS/rate-limit=F-025/F-016, SSE=F-021, SDK=F-022.

features.title: MCP server: search, compile_context, get_effects, capture_memory, explain
acceptance: MCP tools wrap the same domain services as REST
Works with at least one real agent client end-to-end
Tool inputs validated; errors surfaced cleanly
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: apps/mcp (@tessera/mcp) — @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29 McpServer; buildMcpServer(services) registers search/compile_context/get_effects/capture_memory/explain, wrapping the SAME F-007..F-010 services as REST via a TYPE-ONLY ApiServices import from @tessera/api (no Fastify in the MCP runtime — verified). Inputs validated by the SDK (classic Zod 3 raw shapes); results = text JSON + structuredContent, no outputSchema; errors masked into {error:{code,message,details?}} (same policy as REST). explain = compile -> project whyIncluded+provenance+trace (no bodies; pure buildExplanation). startMcpStdio = stdio transport. e2e drives a REAL SDK Client over InMemoryTransport.createLinkedPair (5 tools + clean NOT_FOUND + input rejection). 2 unit + 7 e2e. ADR-0017. Effect E-003 realized (MCP half). Gateway auth/quotas=F-026; bootable process+config=F-015.

features.title: Plugin SDK + plugin-host (discovery, config schema, lifecycle, isolation)
acceptance: Stable contracts for Connector/Processor/AIProvider/StorageBackend/RetrievalStrategy
Host: discovery + config validation + lifecycle + failure isolation
First-party connectors/embeddings use the same contracts as third parties
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/plugin-host — a uniform Plugin SDK ENVELOPE over the existing extension-point ports (does not re-define them) + a host. SDK: PluginKind (connector/processor/ai-provider/storage-backend/retrieval-strategy); Plugin<TConfig,TCapability> = manifest (id/kind/name/version + Zod configSchema) + setup(config,ctx)->PluginInstance whose capability IS the existing port; lifecycle hooks; PluginContext. createPluginHost: register(unique ids)/load(validate config->setup)/start/startAll/stop/stopAll/dispose/capability<T>/list({kind}). Failure isolation (FR-58): invalid config + setup/lifecycle errors -> failed (never throws out / never stops others); only unknown id throws. Heterogeneous plugins type-erased behind one localized cast (no any). First-party dogfooding: filesystemConnectorPlugin + fake/transformers embeddings plugins wrap the existing factories on the same contract; live in plugin-host (one-way dep on ingestion/ai; no cycle; domain packages untouched). 10 tests (7 host + 3 first-party integration). ADR-0020, effect E-016. SEAMS: error (not process/sandbox) isolation; a split plugin-sdk/plugin-host + wiring F-015 via the host are follow-ups.

features.title: Dashboard: global search + Context Package inspector + UX baseline
acceptance: Global search across code/memory/graph with provenance
Package Inspector renders stages/scores/sources from the compilation trace
UX baseline: ⌘K, themes, skeleton/empty/error states, toasts, virtualized lists; WCAG AA
features.notes: Done 2026-06-30: the dashboard now consumes the real engine (ADR-0022, production-real — no mock data in the app). Data layer: apps/web/lib/api — apiFetch (base URL via NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL, default http://localhost:3000/v1; parses the {error} envelope → TesseraApiError) + typed methods mirroring the /v1 Zod schemas + TanStack Query hooks (useSearch/useCompile); QueryClientProvider wired in providers. The interim client is a drop-in seam the F-022 @tessera/sdk replaces. SEARCH surface (FR-41): debounced query, ranked results, each showing provenance — the contributing signals (semantic/keyword/graph/symbolic) with per-signal rank/score/weight on hover; full loading/empty/error states. INSPECTOR surface (FR-44, flagship): task+budget form → POST /v1/compile → renders the ContextPackage — package scores (budget adherence/provenance coverage/redundancy via accessible progress bars), sections→fragments (kind, tokens, score, per-fragment 'why included', provenance signals + expandedFrom), and the full CompilationTrace (per-stage input→output + drops with reasons). Provenance-first throughout (DESIGN-SYSTEM §7). Added the Inspector nav item; replaced the /search stub. Verification (all green, workspace-wide): typecheck (27 tasks) · lint (15) · format · test (web 13 component/unit — client envelope parsing, search+inspector RTL; workspace 27) · build (next build, 9 routes incl. /inspector; workspace 15) · e2e (web 5 Playwright incl. @axe-core WCAG A/AA on home/search/inspector = 0 violations; workspace 15). e2e now serves the production build (next build && next start) to remove dev-compiler flakiness. Effect E-003 advanced (REST consumer realized in web). ADR-0022. R0 UI arc COMPLETE.

features.title: Deployment profile & config loader (Local) with adapter wiring
acceptance: Validated config schema selects adapters/providers/budgets by profile
Local profile wires SQLite+sqlite-vec+filesystem+Transformers.js with zero external deps
Secrets via SecretsProvider port (env/file locally)
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/config — composition root. configSchema (classic Zod 3) + loadConfig(env,overrides) with TESSERA_* overrides (merged per section, validated, fail-fast). SecretsProvider port + env(prefixed process.env)/file(JSON map) adapters; require fails fast without echoing the value. createLocalRuntime(config) wires SQLite+sqlite-vec+filesystem+in-process queue+Transformers.js (zero external deps) → composes ApiServices (memory/graph/hybrid-search/compiler) + Runtime{stores,embeddings,keyword,readiness,close}; embedding dimension flows provider→vector store (ADR-0006); non-local profiles throw (F-023). Compiler corpus seam = blob-backed FragmentSource (createBlobFragmentSource/putFragment, JSON {kind,text,metadata}). ApiServices imported TYPE-ONLY (no api<->config cycle); runnable process bin kept outside both (follow-up). 13 tests incl. an integration test booting the real Local profile (:memory: + fake embeddings) exercising memory/graph/search/compile; real transformers env-guarded. ADR-0018, effect E-014. Budgets validated/exposed (applied at request layer); Postgres/cloud profile=F-023.

features.title: Observability baseline: OpenTelemetry traces + Pino logs + metrics
acceptance: OTel spans across API → service → compiler stage → adapter with correlation ids
Structured Pino logging that never logs secrets/raw ingested content
Core metrics (latency, queue depth, compile latency per stage)
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: @tessera/observability — libraries use the OTel API only; the SDK is wired at the process (startTelemetry, no-op until then). createLogger=Pino+redaction of secret keys AND raw content (NFR-7; stderr mode for MCP); withSpan/currentTraceId (active-context nesting + correlation id); instruments (http/service/compile-stage histograms + queue-depth gauge) + recordCompileStageDurations; startTelemetry=NodeSDK+async context manager+HTTP auto-instrumentation (console exporters; OTLP follow-up); instrumentServices=ADDITIVE ApiServices wrapper (API->service spans + latency; compiler stage metrics) leaving domain packages untouched. Additive (non-breaking) to verified code: compiler TraceStage.durationMs (F-010), buildServer loggerInstance (F-011, Fastify v5). Wired into apps/server (instrument + logger + http-latency hook; telemetry gated by TESSERA_TELEMETRY=1, off by default; logging always on). 10 obs tests + 1 server test. ADR-0019, effect E-015. SEAMS: per-adapter spans, a fed queue-depth gauge (Queue port lacks depth), OTLP exporters — instruments exist, data wired later.

features.title: GitHub connector (PRs/issues) + auto memory extraction
acceptance: GitHub connector ingests PRs/issues (+comments) via the existing Connector port; incremental via content-hash; network only when a GitHub source is explicitly configured (NFR-3)
Automatic memory extraction turns ingested ADRs + merged/closed GitHub items into captured memories via the MemoryService, idempotently (re-ingest never duplicates)
No network in tests: injected GitHub client with a fake + env-guarded live test; workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02 (first R1 feature): @tessera/ingestion extended — (FR-4) github connector behind the EXISTING Connector port (issues+PRs+comments → synthetic paths issue/{n}, pr/{n}; content-hash over mutable fields = incremental/idempotent through the F-006 pipeline; terminal redaction scrubs secrets for free). GitHub reached via native fetch behind an injectable GitHubClient (paginated, typed 401/403-rate-limit/404 → @tessera/core errors) — NO Octokit (dependency-free, per ADR-0015; ADR-0024). Network ONLY when a source is explicitly configured with a real client (NFR-3); tests use an in-memory fake + an env-guarded live smoke (TESSERA_TEST_GITHUB=1). (FR-14) auto-memory extraction = deterministic rule-based extractors (ADR→decision using the Decision section; merged PR→decision / closed issue→lesson) + a DocumentSink DECORATOR (createMemoryExtractionSink) that captures idempotently by metadata.source (adr:NNNN / github:owner/repo#n): supersede-on-change, skip-if-identical, never duplicate; teeSink fans out to persistence + extraction. The memory dep is a STRUCTURAL MemoryCaptureService seam → ingestion gains NO new runtime dep and no cycle. +23 tests (github-client fake-fetch, connector conformance+incremental+provenance+include-filter via fake client, adr/github extractors, extraction-sink capture/skip/supersede+tee, full fs→pipeline→memory integration incl. idempotent re-scan + supersede-on-edit). Effects E-009 (github connector realized) + E-010 (new memory consumer) + new E-017 (extraction seam). Gates green workspace-wide: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (ingestion 48) · build 15. SEAMS/deferred: commit-message extraction (needs per-commit ingestion), LLM extraction, config/profile wiring of a GitHub source (F-015), GitHub webhooks (F-021).

features.title: Temporal retriever (recency / time-window weighting)
acceptance: Temporal retriever behind the EXISTING Retriever interface (kind 'temporal' added to RETRIEVER_KINDS); ranks by recency (exponential decay) with an optional time-window; owns a persistent SQLite index (index/remove) mirroring the keyword retriever

Passes the shared retriever conformance suite; fuses with the other signals (weighted RRF, per-candidate attribution); wired into the Local profile hybrid search and exposed on the Runtime for ingestion to populate
Deterministic + offline tests (injected clock); workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: the 5th retrieval signal (FR-24), fully additive. `'temporal'` added to RETRIEVER_KINDS (single source of truth) → apps/api retrieverKindSchema (z.enum, DERIVED) picks it up automatically; the compiler treats RetrieverKind as an opaque tag (no switch) and the web SignalBadge already mapped `temporal` (--chart-5) — so no consumer changed. createTemporalRetriever mirrors the keyword retriever's owned-index pattern: it takes a SQLite `db`, owns retrieval_temporal(ref PK, ts), index/remove, and retrieve() returns most-recent refs (ORDER BY ts DESC, ref ASC, optional ts>=now-window) scored by exponential recency decay 2^(-age/halfLife) in (0,1] (best-first, monotonic → satisfies conformance ordering); clock injected for determinism; timestamps normalized from number(ms)|ISO|Date, invalid → ValidationError. Wired into createLocalRuntime's hybrid set + exposed as Runtime.temporal (like keyword) for ingestion to populate. +8 tests (conformance + newest-first + decay(0.5 at one half-life) + window filter + limit/idempotent-reindex/remove + invalid-ts + a fusion test where a recent item outranks an older one and carries both temporal+keyword attribution). Effects E-012 (widened; adapter+conformance) + E-014 (profile wiring + Runtime). Gates green: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (retrieval 31) · build 15. Deferred: per-QUERY time windows (would change the shared RetrievalQuery contract → API ripple; construction-time window only here), config-exposed fusion weights (F-020), ingestion populating the index (same corpus seam as keyword/semantic).

features.title: Compiler compression stage (citation-preserving)
acceptance: The compress stage compresses over-budget fragments (deterministic, offline, query-relevant extractive summarization) instead of dropping them, preserving each fragment's citation (ref + provenance) and never exceeding the budget
Compression is surfaced: the fragment's whyIncluded notes original→compressed tokens and the compress trace stage summarizes it; LLM-based/pluggable compression stays a documented seam (F-020)
Unit + integration tests (compression salvages an otherwise-dropped fragment, keeps query-relevant content, preserves ref/provenance, never exceeds budget); workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: the compress stage now COMPRESSES over-budget fragments instead of only dropping them (FR-31), fully inside @tessera/context-compiler (no domain/API schema change). New compressToFit (stages/compress-text.ts): deterministic, offline, EXTRACTIVE summarization — segments text into lines→sentences (conservative .!? split leaves code intact), scores each by query-term overlap, greedily keeps the most-relevant segments that fit the token target, restores original order, never exceeds target, returns undefined if not even one segment fits. compress.ts: fitToBudget→compressToBudget(items, budget, {query}) — whole fragments pass through; an overflowing one is compressed to the remaining budget (if ≥ MIN 16 tokens) preserving its citation (same ref/provenance), else dropped-and-continue (graceful degradation preserved); never exceeds budget. Surfacing (no new fields): assemble uses the excerpt + appends 'compressed to fit budget (N→M tokens)' to whyIncluded; compiler adds a compress trace note (count + tokens saved). LLM/abstractive + pluggable compressor = F-020 (documented seam). +8 tests (compress-text: relevance/order/never-exceed/undefined/deterministic; compress: whole-passthrough/compress-salvage/graceful-skip/never-exceed; compiler integration: over-budget top fragment salvaged as a cited excerpt, package fits budget, trace note). Effect E-013 (compress behavior; no ContextPackage/API shape change → E-003 untouched). Gates green: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (context-compiler 25) · build 15.

features.title: Compiler reproducibility + caching + pluggable stage strategies
acceptance: Pluggable stage strategies (FR-34): the compressor and ranker are injectable strategies (default = current behavior), swapped WITHOUT any REST/MCP API or ContextPackage schema change

Reproducible & cacheable (FR-33): a deterministic compilation key over the normalized request + config fingerprint; an injectable CompilationCache (in-memory LRU adapter) short-circuits identical compiles to the same package (same inputs → same key/package)
Contained to @tessera/context-compiler; unit + integration tests (key determinism, cache hit/miss, strategy swap changes output + key, reproducibility); workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: reproducibility + caching + pluggable stage strategies (FR-33/34), fully inside @tessera/context-compiler as NEW OPTIONAL compiler options — no ContextPackage/REST/MCP shape change (FR-34 'without API change'). strategies.ts: CompressionStrategy (default extractiveCompression wrapping F-019's compressToFit) + RankStrategy (default defaultRankStrategy wrapping rankCandidates); each carries an id folded into the key. cache.ts: CompilationCache port + createInMemoryCompilationCache (LRU, get/set bump recency, evict past maxEntries). key.ts: computeCompilationKey(normalizedRequest, fingerprint) = sha256 of canonical JSON of {task, budget, EFFECTIVE retrievalLimit, sorted filter kinds} + fingerprint {rankStrategy id, compression id, dedupThreshold?, expandDepth?} — deterministic, order-independent for kinds; the fingerprint MUST include every output-affecting knob or the cache goes stale. compiler.ts: options gain compression?/rankStrategy?/cache?; pipeline extracted to runPipeline(); when a cache is present, compile() keys the normalized request, returns a hit VERBATIM (identical package → true reproducibility) else compiles once and stores; rank via rankStrategy.rank, compress via compression.compress. compressToBudget gained an optional compress fn (default compressToFit). +9 tests (key determinism/sensitivity/order-independence; LRU get/set/evict; integration: cache hit skips retrieval + identical package + miss on different request, no-cache reproducibility, compressor swap changes the excerpt, ranker swap changes order — all with unchanged package shape). Effect E-013 (compiler options + key; no API shape change → E-003 untouched). Deferred: exposing the key on ContextPackage/API, fine-grained per-stage incremental recompute, persistent/shared cache backend, LLM compressor (the strategy seam enables it). Gates green: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (context-compiler 34) · build 15. (Also flipped F-019 status backlog-slip fixed here.)

features.title: Realtime updates (SSE/WebSocket)
acceptance: GET /v1/events streams live domain updates (ingest progress + new memories, FR-38) as text/event-stream, with a reconnect hint, heartbeats, and clean per-connection teardown on client disconnect
Events flow through an injected ApiEventBus (composition seam); the memory-capture route is a real producer (emits memory.captured); the OpenAPI document lists the endpoint
Verified end-to-end (a live server delivers an emitted event and a POSTed memory to a connected SSE client) + unit tests for the SSE framing; workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: realtime updates via Server-Sent Events (FR-38) — ADDITIVE to the REST surface, no change to existing routes/schemas or MCP. events.ts: typed ApiEventBus over the core EventBus (ApiEventMap = document.ingested/document.removed/memory.captured; JSON-safe, non-sensitive summaries) + sseFrame/sseComment. routes/v1/events.ts: GET /v1/events reply.hijack()s and owns reply.raw — writes text/event-stream with a retry hint + ': connected' handshake, SUBSCRIBES BEFORE the handshake (no lost event between connect and subscribe), heartbeats on an unref'd timer, and tears down subscriptions+timer on request 'close'. buildServer gains an events? option (default createApiEventBus()); the memory-capture route is a REAL producer (emits memory.captured with lineageId/kind/title). SSE-transport (x-accel-buffering:no, cache-control:no-transform) so proxies don't buffer. +4 unit (framing + bus emit/unsub) + 4 e2e over a REAL socket (listen:0 + fetch stream reader): handshake, an emitted bus event delivered, POST /v1/memory → memory.captured streamed, and /v1/events documented in OpenAPI. Effect E-003 (additive route). Deferred: WebSocket (SSE covers server→client push), wiring the ingestion worker producer into the API runtime (same runtime seam; event types + transport ready), auth/last-event-id resume/backpressure. Gates green: typecheck 27 · lint 15 · format · test 27 (api 15) · e2e 15 (api 18) · build 15.

features.title: Generated TypeScript SDK from OpenAPI
acceptance: A @tessera/sdk package whose types are GENERATED from the /v1 OpenAPI document (openapi-typescript); a committed generate script reproduces them from the live spec
A first-class, ergonomic typed TS client (createTesseraClient) over the generated types covering search/compile/effects/memory, mapping the {error} envelope to a typed TesseraApiError
Verified by a round-trip test against the real in-memory API server; ADR records the toolchain; workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-02: new @tessera/sdk — the first-class TS client whose TYPES are GENERATED from the /v1 OpenAPI doc (ADR-0025). scripts/generate.mjs boots buildServer({}) (doc = static route schemas; handlers never run), captures app.swagger(), writes openapi.json, and runs openapi-typescript → src/generated/schema.ts (committed; eslint+prettier-ignored via **/generated/**; still typechecked). client.ts: createTesseraClient({baseUrl,fetch?,headers?}) over openapi-fetch (~6KB, zero-transitive-dep) with ergonomic named methods search/compile/getEffects/captureMemory/listMemories/getMemory/editMemory/memoryHistory + request/response type aliases derived from the generated paths; unwrap() maps non-2xx {error} envelopes to a typed TesseraApiError(code,status,details). Deps: openapi-fetch (runtime), openapi-typescript + @tessera/api (dev). 5 round-trip tests against the REAL in-memory API over a socket (search ranked+attribution, compile budget-bounded, memory capture→get→edit(v2)→history→list, NOT_FOUND→TesseraApiError, 400 validation). Effect E-003 realized (SDK consumer). Deferred: migrating apps/web off lib/api onto the SDK (ADR-0022; separate UI change), non-TS SDKs, a CI regen-no-diff check. Gates green: typecheck 28 · lint 16 · format · test 28 (sdk 5) · build 16. New deps recorded in ADR-0025; pnpm-lock updated.

features.title: Postgres + pgvector adapters + Docker Compose stack
acceptance: Postgres RelationalStore adapter (pg + drizzle node-postgres) and a pgvector VectorStore adapter, each implementing the existing storage ports and passing the SHARED conformance suites
A Docker Compose stack (pgvector/pgvector image) stands up Postgres+pgvector for self-hosted; the adapters are verified against it

Postgres conformance is env-guarded (skipped without a reachable DB, like the transformers/ollama pattern) so default gates stay green; verified green against the real container; ADR records the choice
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R1 must; unblocked once Docker Desktop was installed): Postgres + pgvector storage adapters under the EXISTING ports — no port/consumer change (ADR-0026). createPostgresStore (pg + drizzle-orm/node-postgres; Drizzle db handle, migrate/healthcheck/close) mirrors the SQLite RelationalStore. createPgVectorStore implements VectorStore over pgvector via RAW SQL (vector(N) column; <-> L2 / <=> cosine; vectors as $n::vector text literals — NO pgvector npm dep, only pg added; lazy CREATE EXTENSION+table; identifier-validated table name; ON CONFLICT upsert; dimension validation). Both pass the SAME shared conformance suites (port parity with SQLite). docker-compose.yml = pgvector/pgvector:pg16 service (healthcheck+volume) — the self-hosted stack. Conformance is ENV-GUARDED (TESSERA_TEST_POSTGRES=1 + DATABASE_URL, default postgres://tessera:tessera@127.0.0.1:5432/tessera; F-005 pattern) so default gates stay offline-green. VERIFIED green against the live container: `docker compose up -d --wait postgres` → 10 guarded tests pass (pgvector 6 VectorStore + postgres-relational 4 RelationalStore/Drizzle round-trip); all 29 storage tests green with PG enabled. Effects E-001/E-007 realized. Deferred (E-014): a full self-hosted PROFILE also needs Postgres-backed graph/memory stores (current ones are SQLite-specific) so createLocalRuntime still throws for non-local; backup/migrations = F-024. Gates green (default, PG skips): typecheck 28 · lint 16 · format · test 28 · build 16. New dep: pg (+ @types/pg dev); pnpm-lock updated. NOTE: Docker Hub CDN DNS was intermittently flaky — the image pull needed one retry.

features.title: Backup/restore + migration system
acceptance: A versioned, idempotent migration runner over the RelationalStore (tracks applied ids in a _tessera_migrations table), working on both SQLite and Postgres (env-guarded PG)
Backup/restore for the local stores: SQLite online backup + restore, and recursive filesystem (blob) backup/restore — round-trip verified (seed → backup → mutate → restore → data matches)
Offline-verifiable tests (PG parity env-guarded); workspace gates green; ADR records the approach; Postgres physical backup (pg_dump/managed snapshots) documented as an ops concern
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (last R1 feature): backup/restore + a versioned migration runner (FR-56), additive to @tessera/storage — no port change (ADR-0027). migrations/runner.ts: runMigrations(db, migrations) applies ordered {id, up} migrations IDEMPOTENTLY, tracking applied ids in _tessera_migrations(id, applied_at) and skipping already-applied; ids constrained to a safe pattern (interpolated); a minimal MigrationDb seam with sqliteMigrationDb / postgresMigrationDb runs it on BOTH backends. backup/backup.ts: backupSqlite (SQLite online backup — consistent single-file snapshot, safe while in use) + restoreSqlite (file copy, clears stale WAL/SHM); backupDirectory/restoreDirectory (recursive fs copy for the blob store). No new deps (better-sqlite3 + node:fs). Tests: SQLite migration apply/idempotent/incremental/unsafe-id-reject + backup round-trips (sqlite seed→backup→mutate→restore→'original'; directory tree) — 5 new + a guarded Postgres migration-parity test. Gates green (default; PG skips): format · typecheck 28 · lint 16 · test 28 · build 16. NOTE: Docker Desktop was NOT running in the resume session, so the guarded Postgres parity test skipped (the Postgres RelationalStore itself was verified live against the F-023 container; postgresMigrationDb is a thin drizzle.execute wrapper). Deferred: Postgres physical backup (pg_dump/managed snapshots — ops concern); adopting the runner to replace adapters' ad-hoc CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS; down-migrations/incremental backup. Effects E-001/E-007. R1 COMPLETE (F-017..F-024 all done).

features.title: Multi-tenancy + org RBAC
acceptance: AuthProvider port in @tessera/api with a local/none adapter (a single default tenant + a full-access principal → today's zero-auth local behavior is unchanged) and an OIDC + RBAC cloud adapter as a documented, env-guarded seam; a principal is a user or a scoped, revocable API token (NFR-2)

Tenancy + RBAC domain model: an org/workspace tenant scope + roles (owner/admin/member/viewer) mapped to a permission catalog (memory:read/write, search/compile/effects:read, admin:manage); the /v1 boundary authenticates every request into an AuthContext {principal, tenantId, roles} and authorizes each route against its required permission, returning the standard {error} envelope (401 UNAUTHORIZED / 403 FORBIDDEN)

Scoped, revocable API tokens via a TokenStore port + local adapter (hashed at rest, scopes, revoke); org/workspace isolation is carried on AuthContext.tenantId and threaded to the request, with cross-tenant data-plane row-scoping (domain stores) + live OIDC verification kept as documented, env-guarded seams (mirroring the F-023/F-015 local-first + cloud-seam pattern)

Additive & back-compatible: existing routes + e2e stay green under the default local (no-auth) profile; an ADR records the auth/tenancy approach and the still-open OIDC-library question; workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (first R2 feature): the API auth control plane (FR-52/FR-54/NFR-2) — tenancy + RBAC + scoped tokens, fully in @tessera/api under new ports (ADR-0028), ADDITIVE and back-compatible. auth/model.ts: TenantId + DEFAULT_TENANT_ID, ROLES (owner/admin/member/viewer), a PERMISSIONS catalog + ROLE_PERMISSIONS, Principal/AuthContext, and effectivePermissions = roles' permissions ∩ a token's scopes (least privilege). AuthProvider port (auth/provider.ts): createLocalAuthProvider = NONE (full-access default principal in the default tenant → today's zero-auth local behavior, unchanged) + createTokenAuthProvider (requires a valid Bearer; missing/invalid → 401). TokenStore port (auth/token-store.ts) + createInMemoryTokenStore: crypto-random 'tsk_' secrets HASHED AT REST (SHA-256; plaintext returned once), verify/revoke/list. Enforcement (auth/plugin.ts): registerAuth adds an onRequest hook resolving request.authContext via the injected provider (skips public routes) + requirePermission(p) per-route guards; buildServer gains auth? (default local none); /v1/openapi.json marked public. Per-route guards: search→search:read, compile→compile:read, effects→effects:read, memory GET→memory:read, memory POST/PATCH→memory:write. Errors reuse the envelope (UnauthorizedError→401, ForbiddenError→403; @tessera/core already had both codes). Because the default is the none provider, every existing route + e2e stays green (unauthenticated → full access). +21 unit (model 8 · token-store 6 · provider 7) + 7 auth e2e (401/403/200/revoked/public-openapi + zero-auth back-compat). Effects E-003 (additive 401/403 paths; response schemas + OpenAPI unchanged) + new E-018 (auth/tenancy/RBAC contract). Gates green workspace-wide: format · typecheck 28 · lint 16 · test 28 (api 36) · build 16 · e2e 15 (api 25). DELIBERATELY DEFERRED as documented, env-guarded seams (precedent F-023/F-021): live OIDC verification (OIDC is just another AuthProvider; the concrete IdP/library stays an open ADR), data-plane per-tenant row isolation (AuthContext.tenantId is resolved+carried, but the domain stores are not yet tenant-scoped — no cross-tenant guarantee beyond the boundary is claimed), composition-root wiring (config/server env → choose provider; a one-line seam, capability proven by the token-provider e2e), a persistent SQLite TokenStore, advertising the Bearer scheme in OpenAPI, and MCP gateway auth+quotas (F-026).

features.title: MCP gateway (multi-client auth + quotas)
acceptance: The MCP surface brokers clients with per-call authentication + RBAC authorization, reusing the F-025 auth model (AuthProvider/AuthContext/Permission) via a TYPE-ONLY import from @tessera/api (no Fastify in the MCP runtime); each tool declares a required permission (search→search:read, compile_context/explain→compile:read, get_effects→effects:read, capture_memory→memory:write) and is denied with a clean masked envelope (UNAUTHORIZED / FORBIDDEN) when the client's principal lacks it

Per-principal quotas: a QuotaLimiter port + in-memory fixed-window adapter (injected clock) meters tool calls per principal; exceeding the limit surfaces a RATE_LIMITED error (new @tessera/core error code → 429 at the REST envelope), metered independently per principal

Additive & back-compatible: buildMcpServer takes an optional gateway (auth + quota + credential resolver); with no gateway the five tools behave exactly as before (existing e2e green). Verified by unit tests (authorize + quota guard) + a real-MCP-client e2e over InMemoryTransport (a restricted principal is denied a tool; quota exhaustion returns RATE_LIMITED); an ADR records the design; workspace gates green
features.notes: Done 2026-07-03 (R2): MCP gateway — multi-client auth + quotas (FR-36), additive to @tessera/mcp (ADR-0029). REUSES the F-025 auth model TYPE-ONLY (AuthProvider/AuthContext/AuthInput/Permission imported as types from @tessera/api) so the MCP runtime stays Fastify-free (F-012 invariant preserved — the gateway needs only ctx.permissions.has()). gateway.ts: createMcpGateway({auth, quota?, resolveCredential?}) → guard(tool, extra) = authenticate (→AuthContext, UnauthorizedError on bad/missing) → authorize the tool's required permission (TOOL_PERMISSIONS: search→search:read, compile_context/explain→compile:read, get_effects→effects:read, capture_memory→memory:write; ForbiddenError) → meter the principal (RateLimitedError). Errors flow through the EXISTING masked envelope (toEnvelope) — 4xx-safe codes surfaced. quota.ts: QuotaLimiter port + createInMemoryQuotaLimiter (fixed-window, injected clock, independent per-principal buckets). buildMcpServer(services, {gateway?}) wraps each tool with the guard; DEFAULT off = the five tools behave exactly as before. resolveCredential(extra) reads the SDK authInfo/Authorization header (default) — transport-agnostic; stdio carries one identity, the multi-client HTTP transport is a seam. New shared @tessera/core RATE_LIMITED code + RateLimitedError → 429 at the REST envelope (statusForCode/codeForStatus/errorCodeSchema + web mirror updated; additive, compiler-guided by the exhaustive switch); MCP surfaces it as a code. +18 tests: mcp unit 14 (quota 4 · gateway 8 · explain 2) + mcp e2e 11 (gateway 4 over a REAL MCP client/InMemoryTransport: viewer denied capture_memory=FORBIDDEN + reads allowed, member allowed, quota exhaustion=RATE_LIMITED, default-no-gateway back-compat) + api error-handler RATE_LIMITED→429. Effects E-018 (MCP consumer of the auth model + quota port) + E-006 (core RATE_LIMITED) + E-003 (guarded tools; 429 envelope; SDK regen = no diff since the error envelope isn't in the OpenAPI). Gates green workspace-wide: format · typecheck 28 · lint 16 · test 28 (mcp 14, api 36) · build 16 · e2e 15 (mcp 11). DELIBERATELY DEFERRED as documented seams: the multi-client HTTP/streamable transport + auth middleware (populates authInfo), a persistent/distributed quota store, token-bucket/sliding-window + per-tool weights + quota headers, and composition-root wiring (config/server → construct the gateway from a profile; capability proven by the e2e injecting the real F-025 token provider).

features.title: Governance & audit UI + full audit trail
UI foundation: design tokens/theming, base shadcn components, app shell, command palette
acceptance: Design tokens (semantic CSS vars) + light/dark/system theming per docs/design/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md
Base shadcn/ui components, app shell (sidebar + topbar), command palette (⌘K)
Skeleton/empty/error primitives, toasts, motion primitives; WCAG AA baseline
features.notes: Done 2026-06-30: apps/web (@tessera/web) — Next.js 16 (App Router, RSC) + React 19 + TS strict + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui, built with the F-033 frontend harness (build-ui skill). Design tokens = semantic CSS vars (OKLCH) in app/globals.css (:root + .dark) mapped via Tailwind v4 @theme; light/dark/system via next-themes (system default). Base shadcn primitives owned in-repo (button/card/input/badge/separator/skeleton/tooltip/dialog/dropdown-menu/sheet/command/sonner). App shell = collapsible sidebar + sticky topbar (command-palette search, theme toggle, user/org placeholder — auth is R2) + responsive mobile drawer (Sheet) + skip-link/landmarks. ⌘K command palette (cmdk) with nav+theme actions, Zustand store, global Ctrl/⌘-K listener. UX-baseline primitives: EmptyState/ErrorState, Skeleton, sonner toasts; functional motion (lib/motion + Framer MotionConfig reducedMotion=user + CSS reduced-motion). Data layer deferred (no @tessera/sdk until F-022) — foundation only; navigable stub pages for search/graph/memory/sources/settings. Verification (all green): typecheck (27 tasks; web typecheck = next typegen + tsc), lint (15), format, test (web 7 component tests via Vitest+RTL+jsdom; workspace 27 tasks), build (next build, 8 routes prerendered; workspace 15), e2e (web 3 Playwright incl. @axe-core WCAG A/AA = 0 violations; workspace 15). a11y gate activated (ADR-0021); CI installs the Playwright browser before e2e. Effect E-004 realized. Next: F-014 (dashboard: global search + Context Package inspector) now unblocked.

features.title: CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions) running the verification gates
acceptance: Workflow runs verify-state + typecheck/lint/format/test/build (+e2e) on Node 22/pnpm
Dependency audit + secret scanning; steps mirror .harness/verification/gates.json
Activates when a Git remote exists; branch protection requires green CI
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: .github/workflows/ci.yml — verify job mirrors all 7 gates (state→typecheck→lint→format→test→build→e2e) on Node 22.16.0+pnpm 9 (effect E-005); security job = pnpm audit --audit-level=high; NEW secret-scan job = gitleaks-action@v2 over full history with a scoped .gitleaks.toml allowlist (tests/examples/docs/redaction-detectors excluded — production source still scanned; verified the only secret-format strings in the repo are in allowlisted tests/plans). Header documents activation-on-remote + branch protection requiring verify+security+secret-scan. ADR-0010 (no new ADR). CD (build/deploy) = R1 image/R2 cloud.

features.title: Billing & subscriptions (cloud) behind a Billing port (Dodo Payments)
features.notes: Direction per ADR-0011; gated on OQ4 (license/business model).

features.title: General-purpose execution harness (strategic-compact, continuous-learning, coding-standards; ECC-adapted)
acceptance: strategic-compact + continuous-learning + coding-standards skills present and wired into protocols (workflow, clean-state, definition-of-done)
Coding rules enriched (KISS/DRY/YAGNI, small functions, no magic numbers, parallel async, AAA tests)
MIT attribution to ECC in NOTICE.md; decision recorded in ADR-0013
features.notes: Done 2026-06-28. Harness tooling (ADR-0013); executable hooks deferred; ECC = standing reference for coding harness.

features.title: Runnable server entrypoints (REST + MCP) wiring the Local profile
acceptance: A REST entrypoint boots the Local profile (createLocalRuntime) and serves the /v1 API; graceful shutdown closes the runtime
An MCP entrypoint boots the Local profile and serves the tools over stdio
Lives outside @tessera/api and @tessera/config so the dependency graph stays acyclic
features.notes: Done 2026-06-29: apps/server (@tessera/server) — the runnable bin deferred from F-011/F-015 (ADR-0018). createServerRuntime = loadConfig(env+overrides) -> createLocalRuntime; startApiServer builds the F-011 server over runtime.services + listen (HOST/PORT, default 127.0.0.1:3000) + graceful close; startMcpServer = startMcpStdio (connected-server type derived via Awaited<ReturnType<...>> so no direct SDK dep). Executable bins src/bin/{api,mcp}.ts (#!/usr/bin/env node, package.json#bin tessera-api/tessera-mcp) with SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown; MCP bin logs to stderr only. Depends on config+api+mcp; nothing depends on it -> acyclic. 2 tests: REST boots the real Local profile on an ephemeral port and answers /health,/ready,/v1/openapi.json over HTTP (offline, fake embeddings); MCP composition. Realizes the runnable-bins consumer on effect E-014. The engine is now bootable end-to-end.

features.title: Frontend execution harness: UI skills, web verification gates, design manifest (+ design-skill adaptation, Astryx evaluation)
acceptance: Frontend skills (build-ui, shadcn, frontend-craft, motion) present in .harness/skills + .claude shims, subordinate to docs/design/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md
Machine-readable design manifest (docs/design/design-system.manifest.json) projects tokens/components/motion/UX-baseline/budgets for the harness
Web verification gates (a11y, web-perf) registered as 'planned' (activate with F-028); external skills attributed in NOTICE.md; decision in ADR-0021
features.notes: Done 2026-06-30: built the frontend harness BEFORE the UI arc so it is actually used. Evaluated Meta Astryx (open-sourced 2026-06-27, StyleX, agent-ready MCP/manifest) and DEFERRED it to an R1 watch-item — kept shadcn/ui (ratified ADR-0009): a 3-day-old public dep vs the production-grade bar, and it would orphan the tweakcn workflow. Adopted Astryx's best idea instead: a machine-readable design manifest (docs/design/design-system.manifest.json) projecting DESIGN-SYSTEM.md for agents. Adapted 4 external design skills into .harness/skills (+ .claude shims), subordinate to DESIGN-SYSTEM.md, attributed in NOTICE.md: build-ui (orchestrator), shadcn (official shadcn skill, MIT), frontend-craft (Anthropic frontend-design Apache-2.0 + Leonxlnx taste-skill MIT — capped by 'restraint over richness'), motion (Emil Kowalski MIT). Registered web gates a11y + web-perf as 'planned' (activate with F-028, mirroring e2e@F-011). Refreshed apps/web/AGENTS.md; ADR-0021. Auth confirmed out-of-scope for R0 (none/local; OIDC+RBAC = F-025/F-026 @ R2, library = ADR @ R2). Next: F-028 (UI foundation) on this harness.
